prompt: Read the text "Cheating" and write one word in each gap.
explanation: Each gap needs a preposition that fits a fixed pattern: succeed in, a question about, learn about, know a lot about, capable of, help someone with, continue with, cope with, an opinion about/on, cheat in.
passageTemplate:
You're doing a history test. Your friend, who's sitting next to you, really wants to succeed {{blank-1}} the test. There's a question {{blank-2}} the First World War, which you've been learning {{blank-3}} recently. You know a lot {{blank-4}} it, but your friend isn't really capable {{blank-5}} answering the question properly.

Your friend whispers, "Help me!" What should you do? Should you help your friend {{blank-6}} the question, or just continue {{blank-7}} your own test?

Every student has to cope {{blank-8}} this difficult situation at some point.

What's your opinion {{blank-9}} cheating? Should you help your friend cheat {{blank-10}} the test or not?
